title: hoya cumingiana subsp. cumingiana
intro: Hoya cumingiana subsp. cumingiana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the waxy or porcelain flower due to its unique, waxy blooms. Traditionally, Hoya cumingiana subsp. cumingiana has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hoya cumingiana subsp. cumingiana include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Hoya cumingiana subsp. cumingiana typically involve infusing the leaves or roots in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
